rulings.Case Title: GRADY FRAZIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute Claim
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Ingersoll Rand Co.¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410-583.430 is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless defendant notifies plaintiff of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant J-M Manufacturing Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the causation issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to present sufficient evidence to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff¿s claim. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration, defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories and the deposition testimony of Mr. Reichert would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing pipe attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s objections to defendant¿s Exhibit I are sustained and to Exhibit J are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and to the deposition testimony of Mr. Reichert are overruled. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiff¿s claims against it. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD TAYON et al VS. AMCORD, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Mission Stucco Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed August 30, 2013. = (503/TLJ)
